Description:
  Manage hotfixes.

Usage:
  zou hotfix [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  start             Start hotfix.
  enter             Enter hotfix mode.
  select <vspec>    Select a bundle hotfix. This command is equivalent to the prod select
                    followed by the hotfix enter commands.
                    - if no version is specified, select the active hotfix.
                    - if no hotfix is found, select the last production version.
                    - if the command is invoked in a submodule, find the bundle version
                      containing the submodule version and select it.
  backport <vspec>  Pick new hotfix commits into development branch.
  clean             Clean hotfix branches:
                    - delete hotfix branches without new commits.
                    - fast-forward prod branches to associated hotfix branches.

  zou hotfix select 0.1.2  Select specified hotfix (hotfix/v0.1.2)

commands.payload:
Description:
  Select a bundle hotfix. This command is equivalent to the prod select
  followed by the hotfix enter commands.
  - if no version is specified, select the active hotfix.
  - if no hotfix is found, select the last production version.
  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, find the bundle version
    containing the submodule version and select it.

Usage:
  zou hotfix select [<vspec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <vspec>  Version tag or hotfix branch specification.

Options:
  -u, --no-pull       Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou hotfix select        Select the last hotfix
  zou hotfix select 0.1.2  Select specified hotfix (hotfix/v0.1.2)

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display info/commits for modules that have changed since last release (vlast).
  - the pub column represents the number of new commits that will be included in the next release (vnext).
  - the wrk column represents the number of new commits that are not ready for the next release.
  - the vlast column represents the version of the last release.
  - the vnext column represents the version of the next release.
  If the -v|--verbose option is specified, the command logs associated commits.

Usage:
  zou ipub [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --alpha <revision>  Release as alpha version.
  --beta <revision>   Release as beta version.
  --rc <revision>     Release candidate.
  --rtm               Release to manufacturing.
  -b, --build <data>  Build meta-data.
  -f, --force         Force a new release.
  -p                  Show only submodules with pub > 0.
  -w                  Show only submodules with wrk > 0.
  -x                  Include external submodules.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou ipub      Log publish info
  zou ipub -p   Log only modules with pub > 0
  zou ipub -w   Log only modules with wrk > 0
  zou ipub -pw  Log only modules with pub > 0 and wrk > 0
  zou ipub -x   Include external submodules
  zou ipub -v   Log pub && wrk commits
  zou ipub -vw  Log wrk commits
  zou ipub -vp  Log pub commits
  zou ipub *    Log publish info of child repos of current folder

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display the list of available SKUs using the following color code:
  - active SKU
  - inactive SKU
  - orphan SKU (the prod branch has no associated dev branch)

Usage:
  zou isku [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -k, --sku <k>       Display only modules that have a <sku> specialization.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou isku -r        Displays the list of SKUs of each module
  zou isku -r -k m1  Display modules that have a SKU 'm1' specialization

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display available wips.
  
  WIP symbol:
      ▼ → the wip has descendant[s]
      ▲ → the wip has ancestor[s]
      ◆ → the wip has descendant[s] and ancestor[s]
      ◌ → the wip has no descendant nor ancestor
      ✘ → the wip can be deleted with option -d|--purge
  
  Color codes:
      ▼ wip:feature-1     → (wip = dev)
      ◌ wip:feature-2[-4] → (wip < dev) of  4 commits
      ✘ wip:feature-3[-8] → (wip < dev) of  8 commits
      ▲ wip:feature-4[12] → (wip > dev) of 12 commits
  
      - feature-1, feature-2 and feature-3 are released
      - feature-3 can be deleted with option -d|--purge
      - feature-4 is not released

Usage:
  zou iwip [<wip-id>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier to display. [default: all].

Options:
  -m, --by-module     Group wips by module sections. [default: by wip sections].
  -d, --purge         Delete wips that are already released.
  -n, --dry-run       Display operations but do not execute.
  -V                  Display all commits. [default with -v: skip merge and zou commits].
  -k, --sku <k>       Display only modules that have a <sku> specialization.
  -c, --children      Display children with same wip.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou iwip -r            Display available wips recursively in all SKUs, group 
                         by wip
  zou iwip -rm           Display available wips recursively in all SKUs, group 
                         by module
  zou iwip -rv           Display unreleased wip commits in all SKUs, group by 
                         wip
  zou iwip -rvmk master  Display unreleased wip commits in SKU master, group by 
                         module

Description:
  List merge requests.
  Use the -v option to display associated commits.

Usage:
  zou mr list [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -a, --all           List all requests.    [default: opened]
  -m, --merged        List merged requests. [default: opened]
  -x, --closed        List closed requests. [default: opened]
  -s, --short         Use compact one line format.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou mr list      List opened merge requests
  zou mr list -v   List opened merge requests with associated commits
  zou mr list -m   List merged merge requests
  zou mr list -x   List closed merge requests
  zou mr list -a   List all merge requests (opened, merged and closed)
  zou mr list -s   List opened merge requests using compact one-line format
  zou mr list -as  List all merge requests using compact one-line format
  zou mr list -r   List opened merge requests recursively in all submodules

result.stdout:
Description:
  Cherry-pick commits.

Usage:
  zou pick <commits>... [options]

Arguments:
  <commits>  One or more commits to cherry-pick. Commit ranges (REV1..REV2) are supported.
             Commits and commit ranges can be specified in any order.
             They will be ordered topologically from less to most recent before cherry-picking.
             Use the -n|--dry-run option to display operations without executing them.

Options:
  -t, --target <branch>  The target branch to cherry-pick into. [default: HEAD]
  -n, --dry-run          Display operations but do not execute.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼     ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose          Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status        Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status     Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color         Do not color output.
  -1, --color            Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all        Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp        Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>       Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou pick foo~1            Pick into current branch the commit pointed to by 
                            the parent of branch foo
  zou pick SHA1 -t foo      Pick into branch foo the commit SHA1
  zou pick SHA1..SHA2       Pick the commit-range SHA1..SHA2
  zou pick SHA2..SHA1       Pick the commit-range SHA1..SHA2
  zou pick SHA1 SHA2..SHA3  Pick the union of the commit SHA1 and the commit-
                            range SHA2..SHA3
  zou pick SHA1..SHA2 -n    Display cherry-pick operations but do not execute

Description:
  Manage production mode.

Usage:
  zou prod [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  enter           Enter production mode.
  select <vspec>  Select a published bundle snapshot.
                  - if no version is specified, use the last published version (vlast).
                  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, select the last snapshot
                    containing the specified submodule version.
  release         Create a tagged snapshot (tag production/hotfix branches).

  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou prod select                Select the last snapshot (vlast)
  zou prod select v0.1.2         Select the last snapshot containing the given 
                                 version
  zou prod select sku/m1/v0.1.2  Same as before in SKU m1
  zou prod select 0.1            Select the last snapshot containing the 
                                 highest version with given MAJ.MIN
  zou prod select sku/m1/0.1     Same as before in SKU m1

commands.payload:
Description:
  Select a published bundle snapshot.
  - if no version is specified, use the last published version (vlast).
  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, select the last snapshot
    containing the specified submodule version.

Usage:
  zou prod select [<vspec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <vspec>  Version tag or prod branch specification.

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou prod select                Select the last snapshot (vlast)
  zou prod select v0.1.2         Select the last snapshot containing the given

Description:
  Create SKU.

Usage:
  zou sku create <sku> [<commitish>] [options]

Arguments:
  <sku>        The SKU name.
  <commitish>  The commitish where to create the SKU.

Options:
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou sku create m1             Create SKU m1 in current module and ancestors
  zou sku create m1 -- sm1 sm2  Create SKU m1 in sm1, sm2 and ancestors
  zou sku create m1 HEAD~1      Create SKU m1 at HEAD~1 in current module and 
                                at HEAD in ancestors

result.stdout:
Description:
  Enter SKU.

Usage:
  zou sku enter [<sku>] [options]

Arguments:
  <sku>  The SKU name. [default: active SKU]

Options:
  --vtag              Checkout most recent version tags.
  --prod              Checkout production branches.
  --dev               Checkout development branches.
  -p, --pull          Pull before entering SKU.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou sku enter         Restore current SKU mode (wip|dev|prod|vtag) recursively
  zou sku enter --dev   Enter dev  mode recursively -- same as dev  enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r
  zou sku enter --prod  Enter prod mode recursively -- same as prod enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r --prod
  zou sku enter --vtag  Enter vtag mode recursively -- same as prod enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r --vtag
  zou sku enter m1      Enter SKU 'm1' in dev mode recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Synchronize branches, tags, and notes and clean up inactive branches.
  An inactive branch is a local branch that:
  - is not the current branch nor the default branch
  - has an upstream branch with the same name
  - has no new commits compared to its upstream branch

Usage:
  zou sync [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -p, --pull-only     Do not push local modifications.
  -b, --rebase        Force auto-rebase instead of merge. [default: pull.rebase]
  -m, --no-rebase     Force merge instead of auto-rebase.
  --no-prune          Do not prune during fetch.
  --no-clean-up       Do not clean up inactive branches.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou sync            Synchronize (pull then push) current repo
  zou sync -p         Pull current repo
  zou sync -rpZ       Pull current repo recursively without displaying status 
                      (faster)
  zou sync * -r       Synchronize child repos of current folder recursively
  zou sync **         Synchronize all repos found in the current folder and its 
                      subfolders
  zou sync ** -p      Pull all repos found in the current folder and its 
                      subfolders
  zou sync **/zou/..  Synchronize all repos containing a subfolder named zou

  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou tag list      Show tags ordered by pointed commit time
  zou tag list -r   Show tags ordered by module then by pointed commit time
  zou tag list -rt  Show tag history through all modules
  zou tag list -x   Show only commits pointed by more than one tag

  Display last version.

Usage:
  zou vlast [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou vlast     Display last version of current module
  zou vlast -r  Display last version recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show version tables.

Usage:
  zou vtable show [<spec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <spec>  A revparse spec for the vtable to lookup. [default: last vtag in current SKU]

Options:
  -t, --format <Json|Markdown|Table>  The vtable format. [default: Table]
  -o, --output <file-path>            The file path to save the formatted output. [default: display to the console]
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼                  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose                       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status                     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status                  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color                      Do not color output.
  -1, --color                         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all                     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp                     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>                    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou vtable show                         Display the last vtable in current SKU
  zou vtable show 1.2                     Display the vtable at the highest 
                                          vtag with specified 'MAJ.MIN'
  zou vtable show v1.2.3                  Display the vtable at vtag 'v1.2.3'
  zou vtable show 61da2b6                 Display the vtable at specified SHA
  zou vtable show -t:json -o:vtable.json  Save the last vtable in JSON format 
                                          to 'vtable.json'
  zou vtable show -t:markdown -o:vtable.  Save the last vtable in markdown 
  md                                      format to 'vtable.md'

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage wip mode.

Usage:
  zou wip [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  delete <wip-id>           Exit and delete wip.
  enter <wip-id>            Checkout wip branches in specified modules recursively and in their ancestors.
  exit                      Exit wip mode in specified modules recursively.
  finish <wip-id>           Release, exit and delete wip branches in specified modules recursively.
  fork <fork-id> <onto-id>  Create and activate a wip on a wip.
  release <wip-id>          Merge wip branch into dev/wip branch.
  rename <to>               Rename wip recursively.
  sku <wip-id>              Create a SKU at wip tips.
  start <wip-id>            Create wip branches in current module and ancestors and switch to wip mode.
  update <wip-id>           Merge dev|source-wip into target-wip.

Examples:
  zou wip start my           Start wip:my in current module and its ancestors
  zou wip fork f1 my         Create fork wip:f1 onto wip:my in bundle 
                             recursively
  zou wip update -r -- sm1   Update current wip with dev branch in submodule 
                             sm1 recursively
  zou wip release -r -- sm1  Release current wip branch into dev branch in 
                             submodule sm1 recursively
  zou wip rename -w my your  Rename wip:my to wip:your
  zou wip exit -- sm1        Exit current wip in sm1 recursively
  zou wip enter -- sm1 sm2   Enter current wip in sm1 and sm2 recursively and 
                             in their ancestors
  zou wip finish             Finish current wip in current module recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Exit and delete wip.

Usage:
  zou wip delete [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -f, --force         Force deletion.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip delete -r          Delete current wip in current module recursively
  zou wip delete -- sm1 sm2  Delete current wip in sm1 and sm2
  zou wip delete my -r       Delete wip:my in current module recursively
  zou wip delete issue:7     Delete issue:7 in current module

Description:
  Release, exit and delete wip branches in specified modules recursively.

Usage:
  zou wip finish [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -w, --wip <target-wip-id>  Merge wip-id into target-wip-id. [default: dev branch].
  -e, --dev-release          Merge wip-id [and target-wip-id] into dev branch and release dev branch to prod branch.
  --major                    If --dev-release option is specified, increment major version.
  --minor                    If --dev-release option is specified, increment minor version.
  -u, --no-pull              Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -m, --no-rebase            Force merge instead of auto-rebase.
  -b, --rebase               Force auto-rebase instead of merge. [default: pull.rebase]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼         ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

  -Z, --no-status            Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status         Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color             Do not color output.
  -1, --color                Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all            Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp            Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>           Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip finish                Finish current wip in current module recursively
  zou wip finish -- sm1         Finish current wip in submodule sm1 recursively
  zou wip finish -w my          Finish current wip into wip:my in current 
                                module recursively
  zou wip finish issue:7 -w my  Finish issue:7 wip into wip:my in current 
                                module recursively

Description:
  Create wip branches in current module and ancestors and switch to wip mode.

Usage:
  zou wip start [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -o, --orphan        Do not create wip in module ancestors.
  -y, --dirty         Start wip in dirty module.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.

  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).

  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip start                Start wip:runner in current module and its 
                               ancestors

  zou wip start my -r          Start wip:my in current module recursively and

  zou wip start my -ry         Start wip:my in dirty modules recursively
  zou wip start my -o          Start wip:my in current module only (orphan)

  zou wip start issue:7        Start issue:7 in current module and its ancestors

Usage:

Arguments:

  -1, --color                Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all            Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp            Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>           Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip update -r             Update current wip with dev branch in current 
                                module recursively
  zou wip update -r -- sm1      Update current wip with dev branch in submodule 
                                sm1 recursively
  zou wip update -w my          Update current wip with wip:my in current module

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage zou-flow.
  
  Environment variables:
    NO_COLOR
      1 → do not use colors
    ZOU_HSEP_CHAR [default: ─]
    ZOU_VSEP_CHAR [default: │]
      horizontal and vertical separator characters
    ZOU_PREFIX_FORMAT [default: 3]
      0 → [module--------head]
      1 → [module ------ head]
      2 → module--------head |
      3 → module ------ head |
    ZOU_SKIP_INIT
      true → skip initialisation
    ZOU_NO_WIP_REBASE
      true → no WIP rebase

Usage:
  zou [command] [options]

Options:
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  --version           Show version information

Commands:
  align <commitish>                Checkout, in all modules, commits whose date is close to the current module head.
                                   It is useful for reconstructing the approximate state of a multi-module repository
                                   at a specific point in time, even when submodules were not perfectly aligned. [default: HEAD]
  attach                           Checkout the best SKU branch pointing at a detached head.
  browse                           Browse the current module in a web browser.
  checkout <committish>            Checkout a commit or branch in the current module. [default: HEAD]
  clean                            Clean up inactive branches (see zou sync -h), delete ignored files or clean dangling submodules.
                                   When deleting ignored files, node, cargo and nuget artifacts are not deleted by default.
                                   Use --node, --cargo or --nuget options to delete them as well.
  clone <work-dir>                 Clone or synchronize a git repository.
                                   - if the working directory exists, synchronize (unless -u option is specified).
                                   - if a SKU is specified, switch to it in specified mode (dev, prod or vtag).
  colors <Logging|Palette>         Display color codes. [default: Logging]
  commit <messages>                Stage and commit changes.
  config                           Configure SKU.
  cosm <submodule-paths>           Commit submodules.
  debug                            Show some debug information.
  dev                              Manage development mode.
  fetch                            Fetch branches, tags and notes from all remotes.
  forge                            Manage forges (GitLab and GitHub).
  hotfix                           Manage hotfixes.
  merge <commit> <branch>          Merge a commit into a branch.
  mr                               Manage merge requests.
  nupkg <framework-major-version>  Generate nuget package versions scripts.
                                   - update the zou/Directory.Packages.Floating.props script with minor or major versions
                                   - then run the zou nupkg command to freeze the package versions.
  oclone <remote-path>             Create a remote locally based on the current repo and redirect tracking to it.
  oreset                           Reset local branches to remote branches, prune tags and branches.
  pick <commits>                   Cherry-pick commits.
  prod                             Manage production mode.
  publish                          Create a tagged snapshot (tag production/hotfix branches).
  pull                             Pull active branch.
  repair                           Repair repository.
                                   - delete redondant vtags
                                   - repair malformed vtag names
                                   - remove oldver folder
                                   - remove orphan folder
                                   - delete orphan vnodes
                                   - restore missing vbranches
                                   - restore missing vnodes
                                   - fix vbranch location
  sb                               Manage zou-flow sandbox.
  setup                            Setup additional features:
                                   - shell completions
                                   - internal tools setup
  shar                             Compute the composite SHA of a SKU.
  sku                              Manage SKUs.
  status                           Display short status.
  stow                             Stow tags and branches.
  sm                               Manage submodules.
  sync                             Synchronize branches, tags, and notes and clean up inactive branches.
                                   An inactive branch is a local branch that:
                                   - is not the current branch nor the default branch
                                   - has an upstream branch with the same name
                                   - has no new commits compared to its upstream branch
  tag                              Manage tags.
  update                           Update zou-flow to latest version.
  url                              Display repository URL.
  vlast                            Display last version.
  vtable                           Manage version tables.
  welcome                          Show welcome screen.
  wip                              Manage wip mode.
  ibundle                          Show registered bundles.
  ipub                             Display info/commits for modules that have changed since last release (vlast).
                                   - the pub column represents the number of new commits that will be included in the next release (vnext).
                                   - the wrk column represents the number of new commits that are not ready for the next release.
                                   - the vlast column represents the version of the last release.
                                   - the vnext column represents the version of the next release.
                                   If the -v|--verbose option is specified, the command logs associated commits.
  isku                             Display the list of available SKUs using the following color code:
                                   - active SKU
                                   - inactive SKU
                                   - orphan SKU (the prod branch has no associated dev branch)
  iwip <wip-id>                    Display available wips.
  
                                   WIP symbol:
                                       ▼ → the wip has descendant[s]
                                       ▲ → the wip has ancestor[s]
                                       ◆ → the wip has descendant[s] and ancestor[s]
                                       ◌ → the wip has no descendant nor ancestor
                                       ✘ → the wip can be deleted with option -d|--purge
  
                                   Color codes:
                                       ▼ wip:feature-1     → (wip = dev)
                                       ◌ wip:feature-2[-4] → (wip < dev) of  4 commits
                                       ✘ wip:feature-3[-8] → (wip < dev) of  8 commits
                                       ▲ wip:feature-4[12] → (wip > dev) of 12 commits
  
                                       - feature-1, feature-2 and feature-3 are released
                                       - feature-3 can be deleted with option -d|--purge
                                       - feature-4 is not released

result.stdout:
Description:
  Checkout, in all modules, commits whose date is close to the current module head.
  It is useful for reconstructing the approximate state of a multi-module repository
  at a specific point in time, even when submodules were not perfectly aligned.

Usage:
  zou align [<commitish>] [options]

Arguments:
  <commitish>  The commitish to align with. [default: HEAD]

Options:
  -d, --descendants      Align descendants modules only. [default: bundle tree]
  -t, --time-margin <t>  Time margin to align with in hours. [default: 1 hour]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼     ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose          Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status        Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status     Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color         Do not color output.
  -1, --color            Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all        Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp        Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>       Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou align        Align all bundle modules with current one with a time margin 
                   of 1 hour.
  zou align -d     Align descendants modules only, not the full bundle tree.
  zou align -t2    Align with a time margin of 2 hours
  zou align <SHA>  Checkout <SHA> in current module, then align.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Clean up inactive branches (see zou sync -h), delete ignored files or clean dangling submodules.
  When deleting ignored files, node, cargo and nuget artifacts are not deleted by default.
  Use --node, --cargo or --nuget options to delete them as well.

Usage:
  zou clean [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -b, --branch        Clean up inactive branches.
                      Do not delete ignored files.
  -i, --ignored       Delete ignored files excluding nested node, cargo and nuget artifacts.
                      Do not clean up inactive branches.
  --node              Delete ignored files including nested node_modules directory.
  --cargo             Delete ignored files including nested cargo target directory.
  --nuget             Delete ignored files including nested nuget packages directory.
  --usm               Delete working directories of Untracked SubModules.
                      Do not clean branches nor delete ignored files.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou clean -r       Delete ignored files and clean up inactive branches 
                     recursively
  zou clean -ri      Delete ignored files recursively
  zou clean --node   Delete ignored files including node modules
  zou clean --cargo  Delete ignored files including cargo target artifacts
  zou clean --usm    Delete working directories of dangling submodules
  zou clean * -r     Clean child repos of current folder recursively

Description:
  Clone or synchronize a git repository.
  - if the working directory exists, synchronize (unless -u option is specified).
  - if a SKU is specified, switch to it in specified mode (dev, prod or vtag).

Usage:
  zou clone [<work-dir>] [options]

Arguments:
  <work-dir>  The working directory.
              Optional if a remote URL is specified.

Options:
  --url <url>         The remote URL.
                      Optional if an existing working directory is specified.
  --sku <sku>         The SKU name.
  --dev               Checkout development branches.
  --prod              Checkout production branches.
  --vtag              Checkout most recent version tags.
  --show-vlast        Show bundle last version.
  --show-shar         Show bundle recursive SHA.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -u, --no-pull       Do not pull if working directory exists.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Register, unregister or list version resources.

Usage:
  zou config version [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -n, --dry-run         Display operations but do not execute.
  -i, --register        Register version resources. [default: list]
  -x, --unregister      Unregister version resources. [default: list]
  --ai <file-spec>      Filter AssemblyInfo.cs style files. [default: *AssemblyInfo*.cs]
  --csproj <file-spec>  Filter C# project old style files. [default: *.csproj]
  --custom <file-spec>  Filter custom resource modifiers. [default: update-version.json]
  --pjson <file-spec>   Filter package.json style files. [default: package.json|*/package.json]
  --plock <file-spec>   Filter package-lock.json style files. [default: package-lock.json|*/package-lock.json]
  --plist <file-spec>   Filter darwin plist files. [default: *.plist]
  --rc <file-spec>      Filter C++ resource files. [default: *.rc]
  --vp <file-spec>      Filter Version.props style files. [default: Version.props|*/Version.props]
  --ctoml <file-spec>   Filter Cargo.toml style files. [default: Cargo.toml|*/Cargo.toml]
  --clock <file-spec>   Filter Cargo.lock style files. [default: Cargo.lock|*/Cargo.lock]
  -r, --recursive       Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼    ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose         Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status       Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status    Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color        Do not color output.
  -1, --color           Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all       Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp       Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>      Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou config version                 List registered resources
  zou config version --vp            List registered Version.props style 
                                     resources
  zou config version -i              Register well-known version resources
  zou config version -irn            Display registration operations recursively
  zou config version -i --rc         Register C++ resource files
  zou config version -i --pjson      Register package.json files
  zou config version -i --plock      Register package-lock.json files
  zou config version -x              Unregister all version resources
  zou config version -x --vp foo/*   Unregister Version.props style resources 
                                     from foo directory and subdirectories
  zou config version -xn --vp foo/*  Same as before but do no execute

Description:
  Commit submodules.

Usage:
  zou cosm [<submodule-paths>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <submodule-paths>  Submodules to commit.

Options:
  --me                   Commit current module in parent module.
  -x, --update-external  Update external submodules with new commits.
  -r, --recursive        Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼     ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose          Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status        Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status     Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color         Do not color output.
  -1, --color            Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all        Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp        Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>       Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou cosm       Commit clean submodules
  zou cosm sm1   Commit submodule sm1
  zou cosm --me  Commit current submodule in parent module

Description:
  Release development to production (pull all, merge DEV branch into PROD branch).

Usage:
  zou dev release [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --major             Increment major version.
  --minor             Increment minor version.
  --ver <MAJ.MIN>     Specify major and minor version.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -u, --no-pull       Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).

  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:

                             (default to 0.1)
  zou dev release -r         Pull and merge DEV branch into current PROD branch 
                             recursively
  zou dev release --ver 1.2  Pull and merge DEV branch into PROD branch version 
                             1.2 (create if necessary)
  zou dev release --minor    Pull and merge DEV branch into new minor PROD 
                             branch
  zou dev release --major    Pull and merge DEV branch into new major PROD 
                             branch

Description:
  Manage hotfixes.

Usage:
  zou hotfix [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  start             Start hotfix.
  enter             Enter hotfix mode.
  select <vspec>    Select a bundle hotfix. This command is equivalent to the prod select
                    followed by the hotfix enter commands.
                    - if no version is specified, select the active hotfix.
                    - if no hotfix is found, select the last production version.
                    - if the command is invoked in a submodule, find the bundle version
                      containing the submodule version and select it.
  backport <vspec>  Pick new hotfix commits into development branch.
  clean             Clean hotfix branches:
                    - delete hotfix branches without new commits.
                    - fast-forward prod branches to associated hotfix branches.

Description:
  Select a bundle hotfix. This command is equivalent to the prod select
  followed by the hotfix enter commands.
  - if no version is specified, select the active hotfix.
  - if no hotfix is found, select the last production version.
  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, find the bundle version
    containing the submodule version and select it.

Usage:
  zou hotfix select [<vspec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <vspec>  Version tag or hotfix branch specification.

Options:
  -u, --no-pull       Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou hotfix select        Select the last hotfix
  zou hotfix select 0.1.2  Select specified hotfix (hotfix/v0.1.2)

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display info/commits for modules that have changed since last release (vlast).
  - the pub column represents the number of new commits that will be included in the next release (vnext).
  - the wrk column represents the number of new commits that are not ready for the next release.
  - the vlast column represents the version of the last release.
  - the vnext column represents the version of the next release.
  If the -v|--verbose option is specified, the command logs associated commits.

Usage:
  zou ipub [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --alpha <revision>  Release as alpha version.
  --beta <revision>   Release as beta version.
  --rc <revision>     Release candidate.
  --rtm               Release to manufacturing.
  -b, --build <data>  Build meta-data.
  -f, --force         Force a new release.
  -p                  Show only submodules with pub > 0.
  -w                  Show only submodules with wrk > 0.
  -x                  Include external submodules.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou ipub      Log publish info
  zou ipub -p   Log only modules with pub > 0
  zou ipub -w   Log only modules with wrk > 0
  zou ipub -pw  Log only modules with pub > 0 and wrk > 0
  zou ipub -x   Include external submodules
  zou ipub -v   Log pub && wrk commits
  zou ipub -vw  Log wrk commits
  zou ipub -vp  Log pub commits
  zou ipub *    Log publish info of child repos of current folder

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display the list of available SKUs using the following color code:
  - active SKU
  - inactive SKU
  - orphan SKU (the prod branch has no associated dev branch)

Usage:
  zou isku [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -k, --sku <k>       Display only modules that have a <sku> specialization.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou isku -r        Displays the list of SKUs of each module
  zou isku -r -k m1  Display modules that have a SKU 'm1' specialization

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display available wips.
  
  WIP symbol:
      ▼ → the wip has descendant[s]
      ▲ → the wip has ancestor[s]
      ◆ → the wip has descendant[s] and ancestor[s]
      ◌ → the wip has no descendant nor ancestor
      ✘ → the wip can be deleted with option -d|--purge
  
  Color codes:
      ▼ wip:feature-1     → (wip = dev)
      ◌ wip:feature-2[-4] → (wip < dev) of  4 commits
      ✘ wip:feature-3[-8] → (wip < dev) of  8 commits
      ▲ wip:feature-4[12] → (wip > dev) of 12 commits
  
      - feature-1, feature-2 and feature-3 are released
      - feature-3 can be deleted with option -d|--purge
      - feature-4 is not released

Usage:
  zou iwip [<wip-id>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier to display. [default: all].

Options:
  -m, --by-module     Group wips by module sections. [default: by wip sections].
  -d, --purge         Delete wips that are already released.
  -n, --dry-run       Display operations but do not execute.
  -V                  Display all commits. [default with -v: skip merge and zou commits].
  -k, --sku <k>       Display only modules that have a <sku> specialization.
  -c, --children      Display children with same wip.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou iwip -r            Display available wips recursively in all SKUs, group 
                         by wip
  zou iwip -rm           Display available wips recursively in all SKUs, group 
                         by module
  zou iwip -rv           Display unreleased wip commits in all SKUs, group by 
                         wip
  zou iwip -rvmk master  Display unreleased wip commits in SKU master, group by 
                         module

result.stdout:
Description:
  List merge requests.
  Use the -v option to display associated commits.

Usage:
  zou mr list [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -a, --all           List all requests.    [default: opened]
  -m, --merged        List merged requests. [default: opened]
  -x, --closed        List closed requests. [default: opened]
  -s, --short         Use compact one line format.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.

  zou mr list -a   List all merge requests (opened, merged and closed)
  zou mr list -s   List opened merge requests using compact one-line format
  zou mr list -as  List all merge requests using compact one-line format
  zou mr list -r   List opened merge requests recursively in all submodules

result.stdout:
Description:
  Cherry-pick commits.

Usage:
  zou pick <commits>... [options]

Arguments:
  <commits>  One or more commits to cherry-pick. Commit ranges (REV1..REV2) are supported.
             Commits and commit ranges can be specified in any order.
             They will be ordered topologically from less to most recent before cherry-picking.
             Use the -n|--dry-run option to display operations without executing them.

Options:
  -t, --target <branch>  The target branch to cherry-pick into. [default: HEAD]
  -n, --dry-run          Display operations but do not execute.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼     ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose          Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status        Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status     Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color         Do not color output.
  -1, --color            Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all        Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp        Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>       Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou pick foo~1            Pick into current branch the commit pointed to by 
                            the parent of branch foo
  zou pick SHA1 -t foo      Pick into branch foo the commit SHA1
  zou pick SHA1..SHA2       Pick the commit-range SHA1..SHA2
  zou pick SHA2..SHA1       Pick the commit-range SHA1..SHA2
  zou pick SHA1 SHA2..SHA3  Pick the union of the commit SHA1 and the commit-
                            range SHA2..SHA3
  zou pick SHA1..SHA2 -n    Display cherry-pick operations but do not execute

Description:
  Manage production mode.

Usage:
  zou prod [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  enter           Enter production mode.
  select <vspec>  Select a published bundle snapshot.
                  - if no version is specified, use the last published version (vlast).
                  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, select the last snapshot
                    containing the specified submodule version.
  release         Create a tagged snapshot (tag production/hotfix branches).

  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).

  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou prod select                Select the last snapshot (vlast)
  zou prod select v0.1.2         Select the last snapshot containing the given 
                                 version
  zou prod select sku/m1/v0.1.2  Same as before in SKU m1
  zou prod select 0.1            Select the last snapshot containing the 
                                 highest version with given MAJ.MIN
  zou prod select sku/m1/0.1     Same as before in SKU m1

commands.payload:
Description:
  Select a published bundle snapshot.
  - if no version is specified, use the last published version (vlast).
  - if the command is invoked in a submodule, select the last snapshot
    containing the specified submodule version.

Usage:
  zou prod select [<vspec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <vspec>  Version tag or prod branch specification.

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou prod select                Select the last snapshot (vlast)
  zou prod select v0.1.2         Select the last snapshot containing the given 
                                 version

Options:
  --alpha <revision>  Release as alpha version.
  --beta <revision>   Release as beta version.
  --rc <revision>     Release candidate.
  --rtm               Release to manufacturing.
  -b, --build <data>  Build meta-data.
  -f, --force         Force a new release.

Description:
  Create SKU.

Usage:
  zou sku create <sku> [<commitish>] [options]

Arguments:
  <sku>        The SKU name.
  <commitish>  The commitish where to create the SKU.

Options:
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou sku create m1             Create SKU m1 in current module and ancestors
  zou sku create m1 -- sm1 sm2  Create SKU m1 in sm1, sm2 and ancestors
  zou sku create m1 HEAD~1      Create SKU m1 at HEAD~1 in current module and 
                                at HEAD in ancestors

result.stdout:
Description:
  Enter SKU.

Usage:
  zou sku enter [<sku>] [options]

Arguments:
  <sku>  The SKU name. [default: active SKU]

Options:
  --vtag              Checkout most recent version tags.
  --prod              Checkout production branches.
  --dev               Checkout development branches.
  -p, --pull          Pull before entering SKU.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou sku enter         Restore current SKU mode (wip|dev|prod|vtag) recursively
  zou sku enter --dev   Enter dev  mode recursively -- same as dev  enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r
  zou sku enter --prod  Enter prod mode recursively -- same as prod enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r --prod
  zou sku enter --vtag  Enter vtag mode recursively -- same as prod enter 
                        [<bundle-path>] -r --vtag
  zou sku enter m1      Enter SKU 'm1' in dev mode recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Synchronize branches, tags, and notes and clean up inactive branches.
  An inactive branch is a local branch that:
  - is not the current branch nor the default branch
  - has an upstream branch with the same name
  - has no new commits compared to its upstream branch

Usage:
  zou sync [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -p, --pull-only     Do not push local modifications.
  -b, --rebase        Force auto-rebase instead of merge. [default: pull.rebase]
  -m, --no-rebase     Force merge instead of auto-rebase.
  --no-prune          Do not prune during fetch.
  --no-clean-up       Do not clean up inactive branches.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou sync            Synchronize (pull then push) current repo
  zou sync -p         Pull current repo
  zou sync -rpZ       Pull current repo recursively without displaying status 
                      (faster)
  zou sync * -r       Synchronize child repos of current folder recursively
  zou sync **         Synchronize all repos found in the current folder and its 
                      subfolders
  zou sync ** -p      Pull all repos found in the current folder and its 
                      subfolders
  zou sync **/zou/..  Synchronize all repos containing a subfolder named zou

  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou tag list      Show tags ordered by pointed commit time
  zou tag list -r   Show tags ordered by module then by pointed commit time
  zou tag list -rt  Show tag history through all modules
  zou tag list -x   Show only commits pointed by more than one tag

  Display last version.

Usage:
  zou vlast [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Additional Arguments:
  Specific modules to visit. [default: <startup-dir>]

Examples:
  zou vlast     Display last version of current module
  zou vlast -r  Display last version recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show version tables.

Usage:
  zou vtable show [<spec>] [options]

Arguments:
  <spec>  A revparse spec for the vtable to lookup. [default: last vtag in current SKU]

Options:
  -t, --format <Json|Markdown|Table>  The vtable format. [default: Table]
  -o, --output <file-path>            The file path to save the formatted output. [default: display to the console]
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼                  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose                       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status                     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status                  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color                      Do not color output.
  -1, --color                         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all                     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp                     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>                    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou vtable show                         Display the last vtable in current SKU
  zou vtable show 1.2                     Display the vtable at the highest 
                                          vtag with specified 'MAJ.MIN'
  zou vtable show v1.2.3                  Display the vtable at vtag 'v1.2.3'
  zou vtable show 61da2b6                 Display the vtable at specified SHA
  zou vtable show -t:json -o:vtable.json  Save the last vtable in JSON format 
                                          to 'vtable.json'
  zou vtable show -t:markdown -o:vtable.  Save the last vtable in markdown 
  md                                      format to 'vtable.md'

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage wip mode.

Usage:
  zou wip [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Commands:
  delete <wip-id>           Exit and delete wip.
  enter <wip-id>            Checkout wip branches in specified modules recursively and in their ancestors.
  exit                      Exit wip mode in specified modules recursively.
  finish <wip-id>           Release, exit and delete wip branches in specified modules recursively.
  fork <fork-id> <onto-id>  Create and activate a wip on a wip.
  release <wip-id>          Merge wip branch into dev/wip branch.
  rename <to>               Rename wip recursively.
  sku <wip-id>              Create a SKU at wip tips.
  start <wip-id>            Create wip branches in current module and ancestors and switch to wip mode.
  update <wip-id>           Merge dev|source-wip into target-wip.

Examples:
  zou wip start my           Start wip:my in current module and its ancestors
  zou wip fork f1 my         Create fork wip:f1 onto wip:my in bundle 
                             recursively
  zou wip update -r -- sm1   Update current wip with dev branch in submodule 
                             sm1 recursively
  zou wip release -r -- sm1  Release current wip branch into dev branch in 
                             submodule sm1 recursively
  zou wip rename -w my your  Rename wip:my to wip:your
  zou wip exit -- sm1        Exit current wip in sm1 recursively
  zou wip enter -- sm1 sm2   Enter current wip in sm1 and sm2 recursively and 
                             in their ancestors
  zou wip finish             Finish current wip in current module recursively

result.stdout:
Description:
  Exit and delete wip.

Usage:
  zou wip delete [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -f, --force         Force deletion.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose       Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status     Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status  Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color      Do not color output.
  -1, --color         Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all     Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp     Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>    Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip delete -r          Delete current wip in current module recursively
  zou wip delete -- sm1 sm2  Delete current wip in sm1 and sm2
  zou wip delete my -r       Delete wip:my in current module recursively
  zou wip delete issue:7     Delete issue:7 in current module

Description:
  Release, exit and delete wip branches in specified modules recursively.

Usage:
  zou wip finish [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -w, --wip <target-wip-id>  Merge wip-id into target-wip-id. [default: dev branch].
  -e, --dev-release          Merge wip-id [and target-wip-id] into dev branch and release dev branch to prod branch.
  --major                    If --dev-release option is specified, increment major version.
  --minor                    If --dev-release option is specified, increment minor version.
  -u, --no-pull              Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -m, --no-rebase            Force merge instead of auto-rebase.
  -b, --rebase               Force auto-rebase instead of merge. [default: pull.rebase]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼         ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

  -Z, --no-status            Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status         Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color             Do not color output.
  -1, --color                Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all            Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp            Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>           Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip finish                Finish current wip in current module recursively
  zou wip finish -- sm1         Finish current wip in submodule sm1 recursively
  zou wip finish -w my          Finish current wip into wip:my in current 
                                module recursively
  zou wip finish issue:7 -w my  Finish issue:7 wip into wip:my in current 
                                module recursively

Description:
  Create wip branches in current module and ancestors and switch to wip mode.

Usage:
  zou wip start [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -o, --orphan        Do not create wip in module ancestors.
  -y, --dirty         Start wip in dirty module.
  -r, --recursive     Visit specified modules recursively.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼

Description:
  Merge dev|source-wip into target-wip.

Usage:
  zou wip update [<wip-id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <wip-id>  The wip identifier.
            Format: [<prefix>:]<name>.
            Example: wip:my, issue:328.

Options:
  -w, --wip <source-wip-id>  Update wip-id with source-wip-id. [default: dev branch].
  -r, --recursive            Visit specified modules recursively.
  -u, --no-pull              Do not pull before executing command. (faster but unsafe)
  -m, --no-rebase            Force merge instead of auto-rebase.
  -b, --rebase               Force auto-rebase instead of merge. [default: pull.rebase]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
  ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼         ⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼⎼
  -v, --verbose              Show more information.
  -Z, --no-status            Do not display status at all (faster).
  -z, --dirty-status         Display only modified or untracked content status.
  -0, --no-color             Do not color output.
  -1, --color                Force color even if redirected.
  -A, --visit-all            Visit all submodules (do not skip excluded ones).
  -T, --timestamp            Timestamp messages.
  -C <startup-dir>           Run as if zou was started in <startup-dir>.

Examples:
  zou wip update -r             Update current wip with dev branch in current 
                                module recursively
  zou wip update -r -- sm1      Update current wip with dev branch in submodule 
                                sm1 recursively
  zou wip update -w my          Update current wip with wip:my in current module
  zou wip update issue:7 -w my  Update issue:7 with wip:my in current module
